summary: Epic Dark Star‑Morning Dew marathon with thunderous bass
blurb: The second set’s Dark Star → Morning Dew stretch dominates the night, stretching to over an hour in some tapes. Phil’s opening “stomp” bass drives the suite while Jerry’s wailing lead guitar stays on point. The rest of the show is solid, though a few cuts of Sugar Magnolia and Casey Jones are less inspiring.
review: On November 13, 1972 at Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Memorial Hall in Kansas City, KS, the Dead delivered one of their most memorable late‑night excursions. After a tight first set that ran through classics like “Box of Rain” and “Playing In The Band,” the second set launched into a monster Dark Star that seamlessly blossomed into an expansive Morning Dew. Reviewers across several audience recordings describe this 45‑ to 72‑minute jam as “scorching,” “phenomenal,” and “uplifting,” with Phil Lesh’s angular bass “stomp” providing the backbone and Jerry Garcia’s guitar tone described as “wailing” and “perfect.” Keith Godchaux adds a subtle lift out of the darkness, while the rhythm section holds steady. The only blemishes are a few slip‑ups on Sugar Magnolia and Casey Jones in some tapes, but they don’t detract from the overall power of the performance. Audio quality varies—some audience tapes are muddy or have dropouts—but Owsley’s experimental mic placement yields an unusually clear capture that lets listeners hear every nuance of the historic suite.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising the Dark Star → Morning Dew suite as a highlight and noting tight flow, while two are mixed but still commend the same centerpiece. No consistent negative comments about musical performance appear, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Owsley's audience tape is rated excellent, with crystal‑clear mic placement that captures the full dynamic range of the Dark Star → Morning Dew suite.

key_highlights: A marathon Dark Star → Morning Dew jam lasting up to 72 minutes
Phil Lesh’s thunderous opening bass “stomp” drives the suite
Jerry Garcia’s wailing lead guitar stays on point throughout the jam
Keith Godchaux adds a subtle lift out of the darkness

band_performance.Jerry: His wailing guitar and perfect tone shine during the Dark Star, keeping the improvisation focused and soaring.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers thunderous, angular bass lines—especially the opening “stomp”—and an outstanding solo that propels the jam.
band_performance.Keith: Leads out of the dark jam with a less ominous feel before Phil’s bass takes over, adding texture to the transition.
